top_signals.title: Moubayed: Strong GCC Balance Sheet Kept Ratings High
top_signals.summary:
Ratings agencies reaffirmed the strong position of various GCC economies, despite the geopolitical tensions. Alia Moubayed, Managing Director: EMEA Economics & Strategy at Jefferies International spoke to Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East and Africa on the resilience of these economies.
 (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: BP Dilutes Stake in Australia’s Browse With 5% Sale to GS Energy
top_signals.summary: BP Plc has agreed to dilute its stake in the A$48.7 billion Browse natural gas project by selling 5% to South Korea’s GS Energy Corp.

top_signals.title: Adcock Faces Possible Antitrust Charges in South Africa
top_signals.summary: South Africa’s antitrust watchdog has accused pharmaceutical company Adcock Ingram Holdings Ltd. of profiteering from the Covid-19 pandemic, according to people familiar with the matter.

top_signals.title: NYDIG suggests $1.3B IBIT sale was whale exiting directional trade
top_signals.summary: NYDIG’s Greg Cipolaro says a sale below market price and giving up millions of dollars for immediate execution indicates a large directional holder exited a trade on BlackRock’s IBIT last week.
